birds.evidence: H.|last2=Brassey|first2=R.|date=2000|title=New Zealand pigeon (Hemiphaga novaeseelandiae) on Raoul Island, Kermadec Group|url=https://www.notornis.osnz.org.nz/system/files/Notornis_47_1_36.pdf|url-status=live|journal=Notornis|volume=47|issue=1|pages=36–38|doi=10.63172/383560hgaaii |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190216153219/http://notornis.osnz.org.nz/system/files/Notornis_47_1_36.pdf|archive-date=16 February 2019}} They can be found in a variety of habitats including in native lowland forest, scrub, the countryside, and city gardens and parks. The kererū lives in habitats ranging in altitude from coastal to montane.{{Cite journal|last1=Clout|first1=M
